name: Can a general contractor help me build an ADU or convert my garage?
headline: Can a general contractor help me build an ADU or convert my garage?

mainEntity.name: Can a general contractor help me build an ADU or convert my garage?
mainEntity.text: Can a general contractor help me build an ADU or convert my garage?

acceptedAnswer.text: ADUs (accessory dwelling units) and garage conversions are very popular in the Bay Area, including Pacifica, Daly City, and San Bruno, but they are more complex than simple interior remodels. A general contractor can absolutely manage these projects, but you need one with specific ADU experience. ADUs involve structural changes, plumbing, electrical, heating, insulation, and often foundation work. There are also zoning rules about size, parking, setbacks, and separate utility meters that your contractor must understand. Your city may require detailed plans from a designer or architect before you can even apply for permits. When you talk to contractors, ask to see completed ADU or garage conversion projects in your area, not just photos from somewhere else. Ask how long the permit process usually takes in your city and what common problems they’ve seen. Costs and timelines for ADUs are often higher and longer than homeowners expect. A pitfall is underestimating the amount of trenching, utility upgrades, and code requirements involved. Make sure the bid includes realistic allowances for these items and not just a pretty interior finish. Plan for several months for design and permitting and several more for construction. Clear communication with neighbors and your city can help the process go more smoothly.
